variants.transponderChip: PCF7937E (Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag2 Extended)

programming: On-board programmable with one working key; or OBD programmer (AUTEL, OBDSTAR, Xhorse)
notes: B111 (Z-keyway / PCF7936 circle+) used on pre-2010 Equinox. 2010+ moved to HU100 high-security blade with PCF7937E extended chip.

programming: Requires professional OBD programmer; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: 2018-2021 primarily HYQ4AA (315 MHz); 2022-2024 HYQ4AS. Both use HU100 emergency blade. Push-button start vehicles only.

programming: Transponder: on-board programmable with one working key. Smart key: professional OBD programmer required.
notes: 2013-2019: mechanical HU100/B116-PT transponder standard. 2020-2022: Smart key (HYQ4AS) became common. Some trim levels offered both options.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: All-new 2024 Trax (new platform) uses HYQ4ES at 434 MHz, same FCC ID family as Trailblazer/Blazer/Traverse.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: Primary key is HYQ4ES smart key. LXP-T004 flip key is XL8 trim only per product listings. 434 MHz (also listed as 433 MHz in some aftermarket descriptions).

variants.transponderChip: PCF7937E (Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag2 Extended)

programming: Transponder: on-board programmable with one working key. Flip key: professional OBD programmer required.
notes: 7th-gen Malibu (2013-2015). Standard ignition key is B116-PT transponder (PN 22984996). OHT01060512 is the FCC ID for the optional flip key remote (NOT a transponder part number — corrected from prior version). Older 2003-2012 Malibu used B111 Z-keyway with PCF7936 (not in scope).

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: 8th-gen Malibu (2016-2024). 2016-2020: HYQ4EA 433 MHz. 2021-2024: HYQ4ES 434 MHz. Both use HU100 emergency blade. Check SPID label for exact frequency.

variants.transponderChip: PCF7952E (Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag2 Extended)

programming: On-board programmable with one working key; or OBD programmer
notes: 1st-gen Cruze (J300 platform). Transpondery lists PCF7952E for this gen; B116-PT / HU100 confirmed via UHS Hardware and locksmithkeyless.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure. Note: early 2016 production may have 315 MHz vs 433 MHz — verify via SPID label.
notes: 2nd-gen Cruze (J400). HYQ4EA (433 MHz) for most trims; HYQ4AA (315 MHz) for some base/RS trims. Both use HU100 emergency blade.

programming: Transponder: on-board programmable. Flip key: professional OBD programmer required.
notes: 10th-gen Impala (2014-2020). Standard ignition key is B116-PT transponder; flip remote key available as optional upgrade. Last Impala sold in 2020.

programming: On-board programmable with one working key
notes: 5th-gen Camaro first gen (2010-2015). HU100 high-security blade with PCF7937E extended chip.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: 6th-gen Camaro (2016-2024). 2016-2020 uses HYQ4EA 433 MHz. Transpondery and multiple aftermarket sources confirm PCF7952E / PCF7961E as the correct chip for HYQ4EA (same chip family as Cruze/Malibu HYQ4EA). Prior entry incorrectly listed PCF7937E which belongs to the HYQ4ES generation. Some OEM 6-button convertible variants (PN 13522886) may use PCF7937E — verify against specific part number.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: 6th-gen Camaro final years. Strattec 5947799, ILCO PRX-GM-4B14. 2024 models may use NXP AES 128-bit chip per some listings.

immobilizer: GM Megamos ID48 immobilizer (ID48 variant) / GM BCM-based Hitag2 immobilizer (ID46 variant)
programming: ID48 variant: professional OBD programmer required. ID46 variant: on-board programmable with one working key.
notes: 1st-gen Spark (M300). TWO key variants exist: (1) DW04CT6/DW05AP keyway with ID48 Megamos Crypto chip — atypical for GM US. (2) DWO4T keyway with PCF7937EA ID46 GM EXT chip (PN 95233522) — confirmed by UHS Hardware and locksmithkeyless. Prior version only listed ID48 and missed the ID46 variant. Verify which variant by reading the existing key.

programming: Transponder: on-board programmable. Flip/Smart key: professional OBD programmer required.
notes: 2nd-gen Spark (M400). Switched from DW04/ID48 to HU100/ID46 ext. Multiple key types per trim. KR55WK50073 flip key confirmed at 315 MHz.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: 2021-2024 Spark uses HYQ4AS smart key (same as Equinox/Trax of same era). Confirmed by multiple Amazon/retailer listings.

variants.transponderChip: PCF7937E (Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag2 Extended)

programming: On-board programmable with one working key; or OBD programmer
notes: 1st-gen Sonic (T300). Transpondery lists PCF7937E. OEM flip key FCC# KR55WK50073 (PN 13584829/13579221) is separate product — not listed here as partNumber. HU100/B116-PT confirmed via UHS/locksmithkeyless.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: Transpondery lists PCF7952E for this generation. HYQ4AA confirmed at 315 MHz via bestkeysolution (part 13529664).

variants.transponderChip: PCF7937E / PCF7941E (Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag2 Extended)

programming: Transponder: on-board programmable with one working key. Flip key: professional OBD programmer required.
notes: 1st-gen Encore (2013-2016). CORRECTED: OHT01060512 (13504204/13500226) are the flip key FCC ID and part numbers — NOT transponder part numbers. The B116-PT transponder uses PN 22984996. Both variants exist: transponder-only ignition key and flip key remote with same keyway. Transpondery lists PCF7937E/PCF7941E.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY procedure
notes: 1st-gen Encore late refresh. Transpondery lists PCF7952E/PCF7961E. HYQ4AA 315 MHz confirmed via UHS Hardware (PN 13506665).

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: HYQ4ES 434 MHz confirmed via UHS Hardware OEM listing (PN 13530511). Also shares FCC family with Enclave/Encore GX of same vintage.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: Encore GX (new model 2020+). 2020 uses HYQ4EA (remotesandkeys PN 13506668); locksmithkeyless also lists HYQ4ES starting 2020. 2021+ HYQ4ES is standard. Check SPID for split year 2020.

programming: On-board programmable with one working key (circle+ chip pattern)
notes: 1st-gen Enclave (Lambda platform). Uses B111 Z-keyway with PCF7936AS circle+ chip. Confirmed via transpondery.com.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: 2nd-gen Enclave (C1 platform, 2018-2020). Transpondery lists PCF7952E/PCF7961E with HYQ4EA. eBay/remotesandkeys confirm PN 13532751.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: 2021-2024 Enclave. Transitioned from HYQ4EA to HYQ4ES. Confirmed via remotesandkeys (2023 PN 13530511) and keylessentryremotefob.com.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: 1st-gen US-spec Envision. Transpondery lists PCF7952E/PCF7961E. HYQ4AA 315 MHz confirmed via Walmart listing and locksmithkeyless.

immobilizer: GM BCM-based HITAG PRO / ID49 immobilizer
programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY. Requires advanced programmer supporting ID49/HITAG PRO.
notes: 2nd-gen Envision (2021+). Upgraded to HITAG PRO ID49 AES — not cloneable. FCC ID YG0G21TB2 confirmed via Amazon listing. Different ecosystem from earlier HYQ4AA.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: 1st-gen ATS early production. Transpondery lists PCF7952E/PCF7941E with OEM NBG009768T. Same FCC ID as early SRX.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; Strattec 5942488 / ILCO PRX-CAD-4B1
notes: HYQ2AB (315 MHz) is primary US key; HYQ2AF (433 MHz) used in some markets. Transpondery confirms PCF7941E/PCF7937E. UHS Hardware and locksmithkeyless both list HYQ2AB with HU100 blade.

programming: Transponder: on-board programmable with one working key. Smart key: professional OBD programmer required.
notes: 3rd-gen CTS (2008-2013). CORRECTED: prior version incorrectly placed M3N5WY7777A (a smart prox key FCC ID) in the transponder row and listed wrong chip PCF7952E for transponder. The B111 ignition transponder key uses PCF7936AS (Circle+ ID46), NOT PCF7952E — confirmed by northcoastkeyless, abkeys, Amazon, and oemcarkeymall. Separately, the M3N5WY7777A smart prox key (Wagon and optional sedan) at 315 MHz uses PCF7952E/PCF7941E — this is a distinct product. Both variants exist for 2008-2013 CTS.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; ILCO PRX-CAD-4B1
notes: 4th-gen CTS (2014-2019). Transpondery lists PCF7937E/PCF7961E. Shares HYQ2AB FCC ID with ATS/XTS. UHS Hardware and locksmithkeyless confirm 315 MHz.

immobilizer: GM BCM-based HITAG PRO / ID49 immobilizer
programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY. Requires programmer supporting ID49/HITAG PRO.
notes: CT4 shares platform/key with CT5. YG0G20TB1 FCC ID confirmed via locksmithkeyless and Amazon. IC: 4008C-G20TB1. Not in transpondery catalog; confirmed via two independent live sources. Battery: CR2450.

immobilizer: GM BCM-based HITAG PRO / ID49 immobilizer
programming: Professional OBD programmer required; no onboard DIY. Requires programmer supporting ID49/HITAG PRO.
notes: CT5 shares exact same key system as CT4. YG0G20TB1 at 434 MHz confirmed. V-Series (CT4-V, CT5-V) uses same FCC ID with colored (red/blue) trim variants. IC: 4008C-G20TB1.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required; ILCO PRX-CAD-3B2 / Strattec 5931852
notes: 2nd-gen SRX (2010-2016). 2010-2015 uses NBG009768T (locksmithkeyless lists 2010-2015; UHS OEM Strattec PN 5931852 confirms same range). Transpondery lists PCF7952E/NCF2951E. Locksmithkeyless and UHS confirm NBG009768T at 315 MHz.

programming: Professional OBD programmer required
notes: Final SRX model years. Switched from NBG009768T to HYQ2AB family. Both UHS Hardware and transpondery confirm transition at 2015-2016. 2015 is a split year (some units still NBG009768T, others HYQ2AB). Same key as ATS/CTS/XTS of same era.
